tasks.description: Create `src/tools/video-editor/lib/bulk-utils.ts` with three exports: (1) `getSharedValue<T>` — moved from PropertiesPanel.tsx:12-19, compares by strict equality, returns shared value or null. (2) `getSharedNestedValue<T>(clips, accessor)` — compares nested objects by their `type` field (for effects) or JSON.stringify (for text), returns shared value or null. (3) `getBulkVisibleTabs(clips, tracks): ClipTab[]` — mirrors `getVisibleClipTabs` (ClipPanel.tsx:37-49): Effects always shown, Timing always shown, Position shown if ANY clip is on a visual track (including text clips — per DECISION-006 correcting the plan's original rule), Audio shown if ANY clip is non-text, Text shown only if ALL clips are text type. Replace the inline `getSharedValue` in PropertiesPanel.tsx with the import.
tasks.executor_notes: Added shared bulk helpers in `bulk-utils.ts`, replaced the inline PropertiesPanel helper import, and verified the new tab helper keeps `position` visible for visual-track selections including text clips.

tasks.description: Add `handleUpdateClipsDeep(clipIds: string[], patchFn: (existing: ClipMeta) => Partial<ClipMeta>)` to `useClipEditing.ts`. This takes a patch factory that receives each clip's current meta from `dataRef.current.meta[id]`, builds per-clip patches preserving nested sub-fields, then calls `applyTimelineEdit(current.rows, metaUpdates)`. Uses the existing `getValidClipIds` helper. Export from `UseClipEditingResult` interface. Wire through `useTimelineState.ts` (add to clipEditing usage at ~line 186, expose in editor useMemo at ~line 207) and `useTimelineState.types.ts` (add `handleUpdateClipsDeep: ClipEditingHook['handleUpdateClipsDeep']` to TimelineEditorContextValue).
tasks.executor_notes: Added `handleUpdateClipsDeep` with per-clip patch generation from `current.meta[clipId]`, then exposed it through `useTimelineState` and `useTimelineState.types` so nested bulk patches are not shared across clips.

tasks.description: Add `handleResetClipsPosition(clipIds: string[])` to `useClipEditing.ts`. Loops over valid clip IDs, building per-clip patches that clear x/y/width/height/cropTop/cropBottom/cropLeft/cropRight (same fields as `handleResetClipPosition` at line 166-183, but using `applyTimelineEdit` with a metaUpdates record instead of `applyResolvedConfigEdit`). Export from `UseClipEditingResult`, wire through `useTimelineState.ts` and `useTimelineState.types.ts` same as T2.
tasks.executor_notes: Added `handleResetClipsPosition` to clear the same eight position/crop fields as the single-clip reset path for every valid selected clip, and wired it through the editor context types/state.

tasks.description: Rewrite `BulkClipPanel` in PropertiesPanel.tsx with tabbed structure matching ClipPanel. Props: clips (ResolvedTimelineClip[]), visibleTabs, compositionWidth/Height, onChange (scalar), onChangeDeep (patch factory), onResetPosition, onToggleMute, onClose, activeTab, setActiveTab. Tabs: **Effects** — entrance/exit/continuous selects using `onChangeDeep` so each clip preserves its own duration/intensity (pattern: `onChangeDeep((meta) => ({ entrance: value === NO_EFFECT ? undefined : { type: value, duration: meta.entrance?.duration ?? 0.4 } }))`). Custom effect editor shown only when all clips share the same custom effect slug. **Timing** — speed (scalar onChange), Source In/Out (scalar onChange). Start time excluded (DECISION-003). **Position** — X/Y/Width/Height inputs (scalar onChange), Opacity slider (scalar onChange), Reset Position button (onResetPosition). **Audio** — Volume slider (scalar onChange), Toggle Mute button (onToggleMute). **Text** (only when ALL clips are text) — fontSize and color using `onChangeDeep` to preserve each clip's content. Text content excluded (DECISION-004). Header shows '{N} clips selected' with close button. Tab bar uses `visibleTabs` for column count. Mixed values show 'Mixed' placeholder on inputs, disabled select option for selects.
tasks.executor_notes: Rebuilt `BulkClipPanel` into a tabbed bulk editor matching the single-clip structure, with mixed-value placeholders, bulk timing/position/audio/text controls, shared custom-effect gating, and deep-patch handling for nested effect/text edits verified by TypeScript.

tasks.description: Update PropertiesPanel component (PropertiesPanel.tsx:150-257) to compute and pass all bulk props. Expand shared-value computation (lines 188-191) for all fields needed by BulkClipPanel using `getSharedValue` for scalars and `getSharedNestedValue` for entrance/exit/continuous/text. Compute `bulkVisibleTabs` via `getBulkVisibleTabs(bulkSelectedClips, data.resolvedConfig?.tracks ?? [])`. Wire: `onChange` → `handleUpdateClips(selectedClipIdsList, patch)`, `onChangeDeep` → `handleUpdateClipsDeep(selectedClipIdsList, patchFn)`, `onResetPosition` → `handleResetClipsPosition(selectedClipIdsList)`, `onToggleMute` → `handleToggleMuteClips(selectedClipIdsList)`. Pass `activeTab`, `setActiveTab`, `compositionSize.width`, `compositionSize.height`. Add `handleUpdateClipsDeep` and `handleResetClipsPosition` to the destructured context values from `useTimelineEditorContext()`.
tasks.executor_notes: Moved bulk shared-value derivation into `PropertiesPanel` using `getSharedValue` for scalar fields and `getSharedNestedValue` for entrance/exit/continuous/text, then passed the resolved values and handlers into `BulkClipPanel`; TypeScript verification passes.

tasks.description: Enable arrow-key track movement for multi-select. (1) In `useTimelineTrackManagement.ts`, add `moveSelectedClipsToTrack(direction, selectedClipIds: ReadonlySet<string>)`: groups all selected clips by current row, for each group finds target track of same kind one step in `direction` (same traversal as `moveSelectedClipToTrack` at line 135-165), if ANY group can't find a valid target the entire operation is a no-op (atomic). If selection spans track kinds (visual + audio), each kind-group moves independently within its kind. Calls `moveClipToRow` for each clip. Export from result interface. (2) Wire through `useTimelineState.ts` and `useTimelineState.types.ts`. (3) In `VideoEditorShell.tsx:50`, change `canMoveSelectedClipToTrack: editor.selectedClipIds.size === 1` to `editor.selectedClipIds.size >= 1`. (4) In `useKeyboardShortcuts.ts`, update `moveSelectedClipToTrack` call to use the new multi-clip function passing the full selection set.
tasks.executor_notes: Implemented `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` with per-kind planning and blocking, exposed it through timeline state/context, and updated keyboard handling so arrow-key track moves operate on the full selection.

tasks.description: Add 'Split at Playhead' to batch context menu. (1) In `useClipEditing.ts`, add `handleSplitClipsAtPlayhead(clipIds: string[])` — iterates over all clip IDs, for each clip that intersects the current playhead time (checked via finding the action in rows where `action.start <= currentTime < action.end`), calls `splitClipAtPlayhead`. Clips not intersecting are silently skipped. (2) Export from `UseClipEditingResult`, wire through `useTimelineState.ts` and `useTimelineState.types.ts`. (3) In `ClipAction.tsx`, add `onSplitClipsAtPlayhead?: (clipIds: string[]) => void` to `ClipActionProps`. Add a 'Split {N} clips at playhead' menu item with Scissors icon in the batch context menu section (after the mute item at line 156-168, before the delete item). (4) Wire the handler through wherever ClipAction is instantiated (TimelineEditor.tsx).
tasks.executor_notes: Added `handleSplitClipsAtPlayhead` with silent skipping for non-intersecting clips, wired it through the editor context, and added the batch context-menu action in `ClipAction`/`TimelineEditor`.

tasks.description: TypeScript compilation check. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` and fix any type errors from the expanded interfaces and new handler signatures across all changed files.
tasks.executor_notes: Ran a fresh TypeScript compilation check against the accumulated multi-select changes; `npx tsc --noEmit` completed with zero errors, so no follow-up code edits were required in this batch.

tasks.description: Manual testing checklist. (1) Select 2+ video clips on visual tracks → verify Effects, Timing, Position, Audio tabs appear. Edit entrance effect → confirm each clip keeps its own duration. Edit opacity → confirm all update. (2) Select mixed types (video + text) → verify Text tab hidden, Position and Audio tabs shown. (3) Select 2+ text clips → verify Text tab shown, Position tab shown (DECISION-006), font size edit preserves each clip's content. (4) Arrow up/down with multi-select → verify all clips move together, no-op if any clip in a kind-group is blocked. (5) Right-click multi-select → verify 'Split at Playhead' appears and works (splits intersecting clips only). (6) Verify mixed-value display shows 'Mixed' placeholder correctly for all field types.

watch_items: [CRITICAL] Shallow merge in applyTimelineEdit (useTimelineSave.ts:236-237): `{ ...existingMeta, ...patch }` overwrites nested objects. All nested-field bulk edits (entrance, exit, continuous, text) MUST use handleUpdateClipsDeep with the patch factory pattern, never handleUpdateClips with a static patch containing nested objects.

[DECISION-006] Position tab must be shown for ALL visual-track clips including text. The plan's Step 1.4 incorrectly says 'non-text on a visual track' — this must be corrected to 'any clip on a visual track'. Reference: ClipPanel.tsx:41-42 returns position for text clips.
[DEBT] bulk-panel-tabs: Ensure text clip multi-selections get the Position tab. This was flagged as existing debt — the implementation must not make it worse.
[DEBT] properties-panel-scope: Crop and transition controls are deliberately out of scope (DECISION-001). Do not attempt to add them.
[DEBT] context-menu-scope: Copy/paste is deliberately out of scope (DECISION-002). No existing infrastructure to extend.

[SCOPE] Bulk Source In/Out: These are scalar fields that apply the same value to all clips. This is potentially useful (e.g., 'trim all to start at 2s') but could surprise users. Include them but note in the UI that the value applies to all clips.

[SCOPE] Arrow-key cross-kind movement: When selection spans track kinds (visual + audio), each kind-group should move independently. Block only if a kind-group has no valid target, not the entire operation.

[SCOPE] Undo criterion from success criteria is aspirational — this codebase has no undo/redo system. The patch factory approach preserves data correctly so undo would work if/when implemented, but cannot be tested now.

[WIRING] New handlers (handleUpdateClipsDeep, handleResetClipsPosition, handleSplitClipsAtPlayhead, moveSelectedClipsToTrack) must be wired through 3 layers: useClipEditing → useTimelineState → useTimelineState.types (TimelineEditorContextValue interface). Missing any layer causes a silent context value omission.

[PATTERN] BulkClipPanel should reuse the same shadcn components (Select, Input, Slider, Tabs, Button) and the same `NO_EFFECT` sentinel, `TAB_COLUMNS_CLASS`, and `FieldLabel` component from ClipPanel.tsx. Consider extracting shared pieces if duplication exceeds ~20 lines.

sense_checks.question: Does getBulkVisibleTabs show Position tab for an all-text multi-selection (matching getVisibleClipTabs which returns 'position' for text clips)?
sense_checks.executor_note: `getBulkVisibleTabs` adds `position` whenever any selected clip is on a visual track, so an all-text visual multi-selection still shows the Position tab.

sense_checks.question: Does handleUpdateClipsDeep produce per-clip metaUpdates entries (not a single shared patch) so applyTimelineEdit's shallow merge doesn't destroy clip-specific nested data?
sense_checks.executor_note: `handleUpdateClipsDeep` builds a distinct `metaUpdates` entry for each valid clip ID from `current.meta[clipId]` before calling `applyTimelineEdit`, so nested bulk patches are per clip rather than shared.

sense_checks.question: Does handleResetClipsPosition set all 8 position/crop fields to undefined for each clip, matching the single-clip handleResetClipPosition at useClipEditing.ts:171-181?
sense_checks.executor_note: `handleResetClipsPosition` sets `x`, `y`, `width`, `height`, `cropTop`, `cropBottom`, `cropLeft`, and `cropRight` to `undefined` for each valid selected clip.

sense_checks.question: Do all effect selects (entrance/exit/continuous) in BulkClipPanel use onChangeDeep (not onChange), and does each patch factory reference `meta.entrance?.duration` / `meta.exit?.duration` / `meta.continuous?.intensity` to preserve per-clip sub-fields?
sense_checks.executor_note: All bulk effect selects use `onChangeDeep`, and each patch factory preserves per-clip nested fields through `meta.entrance?.duration`, `meta.exit?.duration`, or `meta.continuous?.intensity`; the shared custom-effect editor uses the same deep-patch pattern.

sense_checks.question: Are handleUpdateClipsDeep and handleResetClipsPosition both destructured from useTimelineEditorContext() and wired to BulkClipPanel's onChangeDeep and onResetPosition props?
sense_checks.executor_note: `PropertiesPanel` now destructures both `handleUpdateClipsDeep` and `handleResetClipsPosition` from `useTimelineEditorContext()` and wires them into `BulkClipPanel` as `onChangeDeep` and `onResetPosition`.

sense_checks.question: Is the multi-clip track movement atomic per kind-group (if any visual clip can't move up, no visual clips move, but audio clips can still move independently)?
sense_checks.executor_note: `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` computes target rows per source-row group and only executes a kind-group when every group in that kind has a valid adjacent track; visual and audio selections are evaluated independently.

sense_checks.question: Does handleSplitClipsAtPlayhead correctly handle the case where some selected clips don't intersect the playhead (should skip silently, not error)?
sense_checks.executor_note: `handleSplitClipsAtPlayhead` first checks which selected clips intersect the current playhead via the current row actions and silently skips any selected clips that do not intersect.

sense_checks.question: Does `npx tsc --noEmit` pass with zero errors after all changes?
sense_checks.executor_note: `npx tsc --noEmit` passed with zero errors after the batch 1-3 multi-select changes.

sense_checks.question: When editing entrance effect type in bulk mode, does each clip retain its individual entrance.duration value (not reset to the 0.4 default)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Code inspection confirms the bulk Entrance select uses `onChangeDeep((meta) => ({ entrance: value === NO_EFFECT ? undefined : { type: value, duration: meta.entrance?.duration ?? 0.4 } }))`, so clips that already have an `entrance.duration` keep their own value; `0.4` is only used when a clip has no existing duration. This was verified from code, not by manual UI execution.

meta_commentary:
**Execution guidance:**

**Parallelism:** T1, T2, T3, T6, T7 are independent — execute in parallel. T4 depends on T1-T3 (needs the utility types and handler signatures). T5 depends on T4 (passes computed props to the new BulkClipPanel). T8-T9 are sequential validation after all code.

**The shallow-merge bug is the #1 correctness risk.** The entire plan's architecture revolves around the patch factory pattern in T2. When reviewing T4, verify that EVERY nested-field edit (entrance, exit, continuous, text fontSize, text color) goes through `onChangeDeep`, not `onChange`. Scalar fields (opacity, speed, volume, x, y, width, height, from, to) are safe to use `onChange`.

**Tab visibility correction (DECISION-006):** The plan's Step 1.4 says Position is shown "if ANY selected clip is non-text on a visual track." This is WRONG per the gate warning. ClipPanel.tsx:41-42 shows Position for text clips too. The correct rule for getBulkVisibleTabs is: Position shown if ANY clip is on a visual track (regardless of clipType). The executor MUST correct this.

**Unresolved design decisions made here:**
- Cross-kind arrow movement: Each kind-group moves independently (not all-or-nothing across kinds). This is more useful than blocking the entire operation when a mixed selection can't fully move.
- Bulk Source In/Out: Included as scalar fields. Applying the same source trim to all clips is a valid use case (e.g., skip first 2 seconds of all clips).

**What NOT to build:** Crop controls (in OverlayEditor), transition controls (don't exist anywhere), copy/paste (no infrastructure), undo/redo (no system), start time bulk edit (destructive stacking).

**Extracting shared code:** FieldLabel and NO_EFFECT/TAB_COLUMNS_CLASS from ClipPanel.tsx will be needed by BulkClipPanel. Either extract to a shared file or co-locate in PropertiesPanel.tsx. Don't duplicate the effect type arrays — import from `effects.ts`.

**Wiring checklist for new handlers:** Each new handler must appear in: (1) useClipEditing.ts return object, (2) UseClipEditingResult interface, (3) useTimelineState.ts editor useMemo, (4) useTimelineState.ts useMemo deps array, (5) TimelineEditorContextValue in useTimelineState.types.ts. Missing any of these 5 causes a silent failure where the handler is undefined at runtime.
